title: 10 best herbal essential oils for upper abdominal pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for upper abdominal pain are a natural remedy that provides relief from discomfort, inflammation, and digestive issues in the upper abdominal region. These oils work by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive system, and easing cramps. The best herbal essential oils for upper abdominal pain include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinnamomum verum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cuminum cyminum, Piper nigrum, Cinchona officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, upper abdominal pain can lead to a decrease in appetite, nausea, and vomiting, impacting daily life, work, and social activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because its active ingredient, menthol, has a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect. When inhaled or applied topically, peppermint oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that often contribute to upper abdominal discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil's cooling properties can help ease digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and gas, providing rapid relief from painful symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax and calm the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with upper abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint essential oil can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, which may contribute to abdominal pain.

Antioxidant: Peppermint essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, a natural analgesic that helps soothe and relax the muscles in the abdomen.
Leaves: High in volatile oils, which provide rapid relief from cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves from a healthy plant with no signs of pests or disease and cut them into small pieces.
Combine 500g of the fresh mint leaves with 1000ml of pure water in a large glass jar and let it steep for 4-6 hours at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container and discard the solids after pressing on the cloth to extract as much oil as possible.
Transfer the extracted oil to a clean dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation for 2-3 days before using it.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil as a natural preservative to the herbal essential oil and mix well, then store it in an airtight container in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The citral content in lemongrass oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort and pain. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to abdominal pain. By applying topical lemongrass essential oils or inhaling its refreshing scent, individuals can find relief from upper abdominal pain and promote overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemon grass oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a common underlying cause of upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
Carminative: Lemon grass essential oil has natural carminative properties, which means it helps to expel trapped gas and reduce bloating, two common contributors to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in citral, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound, making it effective in reducing upper abdominal pain.
Roots: Containing compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help alleviate digestive discomfort and soothe the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from the garden at dawn on a dry day.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm well-ventilated area for exactly seven hours.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 1 liter of distilled water.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel within one hour.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because its unique blend of compounds, including eucalyptol and bornyl acetate, has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When inhaled or applied topically, these ingredients help to relax the digestive muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal discomfort. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties may also help to relieve bloating and gas, further alleviating symptoms of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol and α--pinene, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
Spasmolytic property: Eucalyptus oil contains compounds that may help relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Antioxidant property: Eucalyptus oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which may help reduce the severity of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that help to soothe and calm digestive issues, making them ideal for relieving upper abdominal pain.
Barks: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdominal area, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by washing it gently in distilled water.
Steam distill the plant material using a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 60 minutes.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distillate using a separator funnel and glass beads.
Store the obtained eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural ability to soothe and relax digestive muscles. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdominal area, while its carminative effects help eliminate gas and bloating. Additionally, fennel's anesthetic properties numb the affected area, providing instant relief from cramps, spasms, and discomfort. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, fennel essential oils offer natural and effective relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil has carminative properties, which means it helps to relax and ease digestive spasms, reducing cramping and discomfort in the upper abdomen.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel essential oil exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of abdominal pain and discomfort.

Digestive stimulant property: Fennel essential oil has a stimulating effect on digestion, improving the movement of food through the digestive system and relieving symptoms of bloating, gas, and cramps associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing upper abdominal pain caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help to alleviate upper abdominal cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems, collecting approximately 2 cups of material.
Steam distill the plant material in a still with a volume of 1 liter for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that forms on the surface of the water as it cools over the next hour.
Filter the oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for future use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cuminaldehyde, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the abdominal area, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, cumin oil has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms associated with indigestion, making it a natural remedy for alleviating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cumin essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as gastritis or ulcers.

Digestive aid: The oil's carminative properties help to relax digestive muscles, relieve gas and bloating, and ease spasms that can cause upper abdominal discomfort, making it a natural remedy for indigestion and cramps.

Antispasmodic: Cumin essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help to reduce muscle contractions in the stomach, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as IBS (irritable bowel syndrome) or Crohn's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cumin roots are often used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as inflammation and discomfort.
Buds: The buds of the cumin plant contain high levels of volatile compounds that can be extracted into an essential oil, providing relief from upper abdominal pain by reducing spasms and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cuminum cyminum seeds by hand or using a mechanical separator in late summer.
Dry the cuminum cyminum seeds in a warm and dry place for at least two weeks.
Steam distill the dried cuminum cyminum seeds using 10 kg of seed per 1000 liters of water.
Collect the essential oil by condensing the steam distillation vapors over a period of six hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil to remove impurities within one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. When inhaled, coriander oil relaxes the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Its warming effects also help to ease bloating and gas, which can contribute to abdominal pain. Additionally, coriander oil's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility can help to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Coriander Essential Oil has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to soothe digestive discomforts and ease upper abdominal pain caused by cramps, bloating, or gas.

Antioxidant Properties: Coriander Essential Oil is rich in antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of ulcers, gastritis, and other gastrointestinal issues that may contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Carminative Properties: Coriander Essential Oil has natural carminative properties that help to reduce flatulence, ease bloating, and relieve spasms in the digestive tract, thereby providing relief from upper abdominal pain caused by trapped gas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to alleviate digestive issues, including upper abdominal pain, by reducing inflammation and spasms.
Seeds: Coriander seeds are utilized for their carminative properties, which help to eliminate gas and reduce discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality coriandrum sativum leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal oil yield.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently brushing off any dirt or debris within 30 minutes.
Steam distill the clean and dry coriandrum sativum leaves using a clevenger apparatus with 1000 ml of distilled water at 40°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids within 30 minutes.
Store the coriandrum sativum essential oil in a cool, dark place away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 1 year at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alpha-santalol, have a direct effect on the digestive system, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can ease cramps, spasms, and inflammation that often cause upper abdominal discomfort. Additionally, sandalwood's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in the abdominal area, providing natural relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Sandalwood essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which means it helps to relax and ease muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of Sandalwood essential oil help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as bloating and tenderness.

Carminative: Sandalwood essential oil has carminative properties, which means it helps to eliminate gas and prevent its formation, reducing the pressure and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain caused by digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sandalwood plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate cramps and spasms associated with upper abdominal pain.

Buds: Sandalwood buds contain high concentrations of α-santalol, a compound that has been shown to possess analgesic and sedative properties, making them effective in reducing discomfort and pain caused by upper abdominal issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried santalum album leaves at peak potency and crush into a coarse powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the crushed powder with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, ensuring a ratio of 1:5 by weight.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid.
Distill the extracted essential oil using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature not exceeding 100°c for 30 minutes to produce a high-quality santalum album essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and glyasperin, work together to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and stomach cramps. Additionally, licorice essential oil's ability to relax smooth muscles may help ease spasms and discomfort in the abdominal region, providing fast and effective relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.

Gastroprotective: Licorice root contains compounds that have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the risk of ulcers and esophagitis, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Soilant properties: The oil has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, and reduce spasms and cramps that can lead to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant have a rich source of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that aid in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from local botanical suppliers or cultivate them in your greenhouse ensuring at least 10% licorice content.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them under running water and gently scrubbing off any dirt or debris for approximately 15 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots thoroughly using a food dehydrator or by air-drying them for several days to remove excess moisture and reduce bacterial growth.
Steam distill the dried roots in a large pot filled with distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 hours to release essential oils and licorice compounds.
Filter the resulting oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate it from any impurities and store it in dark glass bottles for future use after quality control testing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including cramps, bloating, and gas. When applied topically or inhaled, the aroma of sweet marjoram oil is believed to calm the stomach muscles, reduce tension, and ease discomfort. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties help to relax the smooth muscle contractions that can contribute to abdominal pain, providing a natural and effective remedy for alleviating upper abdominal pain.

Carminative property: Marjoram essential oil has carminative properties, which help to ease digestive discomfort, reduce flatulence, and alleviate spasms in the abdominal muscles, further contributing to its effectiveness in relieving upper abdominal pain.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sweet marjoram are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with upper abdominal issues.
Flowers: Sweet marjoram flowers contain carotenoids and flavonoids that have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in reducing pain and cramping in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency and aroma for optimal oil production.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or air drying method at room temperature for 24 hours to prevent spoilage and preserve essential oils.
Combine one part dried origanum majorana leaves with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a clean glass jar for a 1:2 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep for six hours at room temperature allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids after storage at room temperature for two weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil, derived from the Melissa officinalis plant, contains compounds like citral and geraniol that have a calming effect on the digestive system. When inhaled or applied topically, lemon balm essential oil can help reduce cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as IBS, gastritis, or stress-induced digestive issues. It promotes relaxation, reduces inflammation, and eases digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemon balm essential oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon balm essential oil may help to reduce swelling and irritation in the abdominal area, which can alleviate discomfort and pain.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate upper abdominal pain due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain high concentrations of citral, a natural antispasmodic agent that helps to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or evening for optimal oil quality and flavor retention.
Steam distill fresh melissa officinalis leaves using 1 kilogram of plant material per liter of water at 100°c for one hour.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from water using a condenser and separator apparatus with a yield of 0.5-1%.
Filter the crude essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and sediment, then transfer to dark glass bottles.
Store the finished melissa officinalis essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to two years.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upper abdominal pain with herbal essential oils is by blending Peppermint, Ginger, and Fennel essential oils in a carrier oil. Apply the blend topically to the affected area or inhale it through steam inhalation to alleviate cramps, bloating, and discomfort. Regular use can help soothe digestive issues and provide natural relief from upper abdominal pain.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upper abdominal pain that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and cramps. Peppermint oil is often used to alleviate these symptoms due to its calming properties, while ginger oil has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe the stomach. Other essential oils like lavender and fennel have also been traditionally used to treat various abdominal complaints, including spasms and gas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the digestive system. The oil contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive system, the oil can help to soothe upper abdominal pain and promote a sense of calm and well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues and promote overall health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the upper abdominal region, which alleviates pain and discomfort.
Shogaols: Act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the upper abdominal area, and also help to relax the digestive system.
Caryophyllene: Has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort in the upper abdominal region by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part for essential oil production, as it contains a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that help alleviate pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Used to produce essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and ease upper abdominal pain through their high content of sesquiterpenes and gingerols.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale contain a higher concentration of volatile oils, which are used to create essential oils that help to soothe upper abdominal pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of zingiber officinale when mature and dry them completely in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried roots at 100-110 degrees celsius to produce a yellowish essential oil with a woody spicy aroma.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid to preserve its potency.
Label and date the bottle and place it in a cool dark place to maintain the quality of the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Turmeric, the active ingredient in Curcuma longa, contains a compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The oil has natural carminative properties, helping to release trapped gas and ease discomfort in the upper abdomen. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy digestive system and relieving upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
Beta-Eudesmol: It helps with upper abdominal pain by relaxing the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause pain and discomfort.
Beta-Sesquiphellandrene: It helps with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and enzymes that can cause pain and inflammation in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for essential oil production because they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a compound responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to produce essential oils that help alleviate upper abdominal pain due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.

Stems: Stems are another part used for essential oil production, as they contain essential oils that help reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Steam distill 1 kg of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes with 4 liters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Collect the distillate and filter it through a glass wool filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered distillate to a separating funnel and allow it to separate into essential oil and hydrosol for 2 hours.
Carefully decant the essential oil from the separating funnel into a clean glass container without disturbing the hydrosol.
Store the obtained curcuma longa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural carminative properties. The oil's active compounds, including anethole and fenchone, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to calm digestive issues and ease cramping. Additionally, its soothing aroma can help to relax the mind and reduce stress, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of calm. remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Helps with upper abdominal pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort in the abdominal region.
Ferulic acid: Relieves upper abdominal pain by acting as an antioxidant, protecting the stomach lining from oxidative stress and reducing inflammation.
Anethole: Provides relief from upper abdominal pain by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain and inflammation in the abdominal area.

Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high anethole content, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can help soothe upper abdominal discomfort.

Buds: Fennel buds, also known as young flower stalks, are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of anethole and other volatile compounds that can help ease upper abdominal pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of foeniculum vulgare seeds when they are fully ripe and dry.
Steam distill the seeds at 80-100°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or a centrifuge.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 20-25°c for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which work to relax and soothe the digestive muscles. Limonene: Limonene has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, helping to alleviate cramps and spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 kg of cinnamomum verum bark at the optimal stage of maturity typically during the spring season.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at a temperature of 35-45°c for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill 500g of dried cinnamomum verum bark in a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested roots at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator.
Filter the extracted oil through a 0.2 μm filter to remove impurities and improve quality.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles at 4°c to preserve its properties for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cuminum cyminum seeds at full maturity for optimal oil content and potency.
Dry the seeds in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Steam distill 100g of dried seeds with 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation and filter through a paper filter.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid immediately.

The oil contains compounds like piperine, which aids in digestion and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, piper nigrum essential oil has been traditionally used to relieve bloating and gas, which can contribute to upper abdominal discomfort. Its natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties also help to calm digestive muscle spasms and ease pain in the upper abdomen.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: It helps to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
Safrole: It acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
Caryophyllene: It has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to relieve upper abdominal pain.
Leaves: Used for their digestive and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe upper abdominal discomfort.
Fruits (specifically berries): Used for their carminative and digestive properties to ease upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest piper nigrum berries in late summer when they are ripe and have turned dark brown color.
Steam distill 200g of harvested berries in 1 liter of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Label the bottle with the date of distillation and the name piper nigrum essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because of its unique properties. The oils contain compounds like quinine, which possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to ease discomfort and reduce inflammation in the abdominal region. Additionally, the oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Cinchona officinalis essential oils may help alleviate upper abdominal pain and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain signals in the nervous system.
Quinidine: This compound assists in alleviating upper abdominal pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the digestive tract.
Cinchonine: It helps with upper abdominal pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which in turn reduces discomfort and pain in the upper abdominal region.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their high content of alkaloids, specifically quinine, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are used to create essential oils for their quinine and other alkaloid content, which helps alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Flowers: Cinchona officinalis flowers are used in the production of essential oils for their medicinal properties, including the presence of quinine and other alkaloids that help soothe abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the cinchona officinalis leaves when they are young and the plant is in full growth at 6-8 am.
Steam distill the leaves in a still at 3-4 bar pressure for 4-6 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the distilled oil and allow it to settle for 2-3 hours to separate from impurities.
Filter the oil through activated charcoal to remove any remaining impurities and improve its quality.
Store the cinchona officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from pain and discomfort. The oil's citrusy scent also has a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can contribute to abdominal pain. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus essential oils may help alleviate upper abdominal pain and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and pain in the upper abdominal region.
Geraniol: Exhibits antispasmodic properties, relaxing the muscles in the upper abdominal area and reducing cramping and spasms.
Limonene: Possesses a calming effect, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to upper abdominal pain, while also exhibiting antispasmodic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils for Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus contain oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdomen.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cymbopogon citratus are also used to produce essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help ease upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus grass at dawn when the dew is still present and the oil glands are full.
Dry the harvested grass in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried grass using a steam distillation apparatus at 100-110°c for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil and impurities in a condenser and separate them from the distillate using a separator.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and achieve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains compounds like menthol and limonene that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from cramps and discomfort. Additionally, mentha oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe digestive issues and ease spasms, making it a popular natural remedy for alleviating upper abdominal pain. Its benefits also help with nausea and indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Acts as an antispasmodic and carminative, helping to relax the muscles in the upper abdomen and reduce gas and bloating that can contribute to pain.

Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating pain and discomfort associated with conditions like gastritis or irritable bowel syndrome.
Menthone: Possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from pain and inflammation in the upper abdomen, as well as helping to reduce spasms and cramping.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Stems: They are used to make essential oils as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to the leaves, which helps to relax the muscles and ease upper abdominal discomfort.

(Optional) Rhyzomes: They are used to make essential oils as they contain a rich concentration of menthol and menthone, which helps to soothe the digestive system and alleviate upper abdominal pain, but their use is less common compared to leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at peak potency when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the fresh plant material in a ratio of 1 part plant to 3 parts water for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled water vapor and condense it into a liquid using a cooled condenser coil.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decanting the oil into a separate container after 24 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities and debris.
